metadata.visit_title: Thoracic tumor board — stage IIIA NSCLC, EGFR-mutant
metadata.note: SYNTHETIC. Authored in the shape of Abridge's synthetic-ambient-fhir-25 contract (patient_context + encounter_fhir.related_resources grouped by FHIR type; transcript as a string). Content is fictional oncology.

patient_context.longitudinal_summary: 69yo woman with newly diagnosed locally advanced NSCLC on a background of severe COPD (home O2). Prior DVT on warfarin. Goals-of-care last documented 2024-09 favoring quality-of-life-focused care.

goals_of_care.summary: Preferred quality-of-life-focused care; declined aggressive measures at a prior (pre-cancer) discussion.

DiagnosticReport.conclusion: 3.5cm RUL mass with N1 nodal involvement; no distant disease.

transcript:
ONCOLOGIST: Let's discuss Jordan Rivera, 69, newly diagnosed stage IIIA NSCLC adenocarcinoma, T3 N1 M0.
PATHOLOGIST: Adenocarcinoma confirmed. Molecular panel back: PD-L1 is 70 percent, ALK negative.
ONCOLOGIST: Given the high PD-L1 I'd lean toward concurrent chemoradiation followed by immunotherapy consolidation.
RADIOLOGIST: Imaging supports N1 nodal involvement, no distant disease. Suitable for radiation planning.
SURGEON: This is potentially resectable. I'd put a lobectomy on the table as the definitive option.
ONCOLOGIST: Okay, let's go with the radiotherapy-based approach over surgery. Radiation oncology to plan this week.
NURSE_COORDINATOR: I'll schedule the radiation planning CT and set up the immunotherapy consult.
ONCOLOGIST: Good. I think that's our plan. Anything else? No? Let's move to the next case.
note: Thoracic tumor board. Stage IIIA NSCLC adenocarcinoma, T3N1M0. PD-L1 70%, ALK negative. Plan: concurrent chemoradiation with immunotherapy consolidation; radiotherapy chosen over surgical resection.
after_visit_summary: The team reviewed your lung cancer and plans radiation with chemotherapy, followed by immunotherapy.
